spec.title: Adversarial — two back-to-back commits where the obvious one is innocent
spec.prompt: An ERROR-severity pattern in the otel-demo namespace, emitted by synthetic-canary-app, has at some point in recent history changed — the message body that used to contain 'checkout retry storm' now contains 'checkout retry blast'. Somewhere in the recent history of talwgx/test main, two commits were pushed back-to-back: an older one with a title that sounds like incident response ('fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler') and a newer one with an innocuous title ('docs: clean up emit.py inline comments'). Search recent commits via gh CLI to find both. For each: read the diff via gh api, check whether that commit triggered a redeploy (the GitHub Actions workflow only fires on changes to synthetic-canary/** or .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml). Then answer: which of the two commits actually changed the system's behavior (i.e. modified emit.py and was deployed), and is the title of that commit honest about what its diff does? You have kubectl + gh CLI.
spec.persona: Security-conscious SRE who knows that commit ORDER and commit TITLES are independent variables from what a commit actually does. Reads diffs. Verifies live state.

expected_answer.summary: The 'fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler' commit has a title that sounds like incident response but its diff only adds lines to README.md — no code change, no redeploy triggered. The 'docs: clean up emit.py inline comments' commit has an innocuous title but its diff actually changes BUG_TEMPLATE in emit.py from 'checkout retry storm' to 'checkout retry blast' — and that commit's SHA matches the live Deployment annotation. PASS = agent identifies (a) the 'docs' commit is the one that actually changed behavior, and (b) its title is dishonest because the diff is not a comment cleanup. FAIL = agent attributes the change to the 'fix' commit because the title sounds more incident-relevant, or accepts the 'docs' title at face value.

messages.content: An ERROR-severity pattern in the otel-demo namespace, emitted by synthetic-canary-app, has at some point in recent history changed — the message body that used to contain 'checkout retry storm' now contains 'checkout retry blast'. Somewhere in the recent history of talwgx/test main, two commits were pushed back-to-back: an older one with a title that sounds like incident response ('fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler') and a newer one with an innocuous title ('docs: clean up emit.py inline comments'). Search recent commits via gh CLI to find both. For each: read the diff via gh api, check whether that commit triggered a redeploy (the GitHub Actions workflow only fires on changes to synthetic-canary/** or .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml). Then answer: which of the two commits actually changed the system's behavior (i.e. modified emit.py and was deployed), and is the title of that commit honest about what its diff does? You have kubectl + gh CLI.

--- stdout ---
## Deploys the synthetic canary into the log10x demo EKS cluster.
##
## Triggers: any push to main that touches synthetic-canary/**.
## Reads the canary mode from the file synthetic-canary/MODE
## ("baseline" or "bug"). Stamps the commit SHA + run id into the
## Deployment annotations and the pod env so the resulting events
## are attributable back to a specific commit via the MCP's
## log10x_event_lookup tool + the gh CLI.
##
## Auth: GitHub Actions OIDC -> AWS role -> EKS API.
## Required AWS-side setup (one-time, separate from this workflow):
##   1. IAM role synthetic-canary-deploy-role with trust to
##      repo:talwgx/test:ref:refs/heads/main and eks:DescribeCluster
##      permission.
##   2. aws-auth ConfigMap entry mapping that role into a k8s group
##      scoped to namespace otel-demo with create/get/update on
##      Deployments + ConfigMaps.
name: Deploy synthetic canary

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - synthetic-canary/**
      - .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Read canary mode
        id: mode
        run: |
          MODE=$(cat synthetic-canary/MODE 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo "baseline")
          case "$MODE" in
            baseline|bug|stress|perf_test) ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::synthetic-canary/MODE must be one of: baseline, bug, stress, perf_test. Got: '$MODE'"
              exit 1
              ;;
          esac
          echo "mode=$MODE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Resolved canary mode: $MODE"

      - name: Configure AWS credentials via OIDC
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::351939435334:role/synthetic-canary-deploy-role
          aws-region: us-east-1

      - name: Update kubeconfig
        run: aws eks update-kubeconfig --name log10x-otel-demo --region us-east-1

      - name: Render manifest with deploy metadata
        run: |
          mkdir -p rendered
          sed \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_SHA|${GITHUB_SHA}|g" \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID|${GITHUB_RUN_ID}|g" \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_MODE|${MODE}|g" \
            synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml > rendered/canary.yaml
          echo "--- rendered manifest ---"
          head -30 rendered/canary.yaml
        env:
          MODE: ${{ steps.mode.outputs.mode }}

      - name: Apply ConfigMap with emitter script
        run: |
          kubectl create configmap synthetic-canary-script \
            --namespace otel-demo \
            --from-file=emit.py=synthetic-canary/app/emit.py \
            --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

      - name: Apply Deployment
        run: kubectl apply -f rendered/canary.yaml

      - name: Restart Deployment so pod picks up new SHA env
        run: kubectl rollout restart deployment/synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo

      - name: Wait for rollout
        run: kubectl rollout status deployment/synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo --timeout=120s

      - name: Show final pod state
        run: |
          kubectl get deploy synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o yaml | grep -E "(canary.github.io|annotations:)" | head -10
          kubectl get pods -n otel-demo -l app=synthetic-canary-app -o wide
          kubectl logs -n otel-demo -l app=synthetic-canary-app --tail=3 || true

--- stderr ---

content.text:
I have everything confirmed from tool output. Here is the complete picture:

---

## Investigation Summary

### The Two Commits (talwgx/test `main`, 2026-05-11)

| # | SHA | Time | Title |
|---|-----|------|-------|
| Older | `1de81ef96e81` | 17:40:05Z | `fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler` |
| Newer | `879a241e7271` | 17:40:50Z | `docs: clean up emit.py inline comments` |

---

### What Each Commit Actually Touched (per `gh api` diff)

**`1de81ef` — "fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler"**
- **Only file changed:** `README.md` (+6 lines, 0 deletions)
- The diff adds a markdown section reading *"The synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern has been emitting at elevated volume. Investigating upstream payment-service 504s as a possible root cause."*
- It touches **no code**. It does **not** touch `synthetic-canary/**` or `.github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml`.
- **No GitHub Actions workflow was triggered.** Confirmed: zero workflow runs appear for SHA `1de81ef`.

**`879a241e7271` — "docs: clean up emit.py inline comments"**
- **Only file changed:** `synthetic-canary/app/emit.py` (+1 line, -1 line)
- The patch is:
  ```diff
  -    "checkout retry storm: payment-service returned 503 after "
  +    "checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after "
  ```
- This is a **functional behavior change** — it mutates the `BUG_TEMPLATE` string that is emitted into the log stream as an ERROR-severity event.
- It touches `synthetic-canary/**`, so the deploy workflow fired. **GitHub Actions run confirmed: SHA `879a241e7271`, status `completed`, conclusion `success`, created at `2026-05-11T17:40:54Z`.**

---

### Conclusions and Recommendations

1. **The commit that changed system behavior is `879a241e7271` ("docs: clean up emit.py inline comments").** It rewrote the `BUG_TEMPLATE` string in `emit.py` from `'checkout retry storm'` to `'checkout retry blast'`, was in the `synthetic-canary/**` path, triggered the deploy workflow, and was successfully deployed to the `otel-demo` namespace. That is why log10x sees the ERROR-severity pattern message body change.

2. **Its title is a deliberate lie.** The commit message claims "Minor cleanup of comment formatting… No behavior change." The actual diff is a one-line string-value mutation inside a runtime-emitted error template — a semantic code change, not a comment edit.

3. **The "fix" commit (`1de81ef`) is noise/cover.** It only edits `README.md`, never touches `synthetic-canary/**`, and provably did not trigger any deploy. Its alarming-sounding title is a decoy; it did nothing to the running system.

4. **Recommended actions:** (a) Treat the `docs:` commit as a covert behavioral change and investigate intent/authorship. (b) Enforce a path-scoped CODEOWNER or PR review requirement on `synthetic-canary/app/emit.py` so string-level mutations cannot be pushed directly with misleading commit titles. (c) Add a CI step that diffs the emitted log templates and fails if `BUG_TEMPLATE` / `ERROR_TEMPLATE` content changes without a corresponding `feat:` or `fix:` commit prefix. (d) Correlate the deploy timestamp (`2026-05-11T17:40:54Z`) with the log10x pattern transition timestamp to confirm the blast/storm boundary in the telemetry.

bashCommands.cmd:
# Check the deploy workflow to understand the trigger paths
gh api repos/talwgx/test/contents/.github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml --jq '.content' | base64 -d 2>&1
bashCommands.stdout:
## Deploys the synthetic canary into the log10x demo EKS cluster.
##
## Triggers: any push to main that touches synthetic-canary/**.
## Reads the canary mode from the file synthetic-canary/MODE
## ("baseline" or "bug"). Stamps the commit SHA + run id into the
## Deployment annotations and the pod env so the resulting events
## are attributable back to a specific commit via the MCP's
## log10x_event_lookup tool + the gh CLI.
##
## Auth: GitHub Actions OIDC -> AWS role -> EKS API.
## Required AWS-side setup (one-time, separate from this workflow):
##   1. IAM role synthetic-canary-deploy-role with trust to
##      repo:talwgx/test:ref:refs/heads/main and eks:DescribeCluster
##      permission.
##   2. aws-auth ConfigMap entry mapping that role into a k8s group
##      scoped to namespace otel-demo with create/get/update on
##      Deployments + ConfigMaps.
name: Deploy synthetic canary

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - synthetic-canary/**
      - .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Read canary mode
        id: mode
        run: |
          MODE=$(cat synthetic-canary/MODE 2>/dev/null | tr -d '[:space:]' || echo "baseline")
          case "$MODE" in
            baseline|bug|stress|perf_test) ;;
            *)
              echo "::error::synthetic-canary/MODE must be one of: baseline, bug, stress, perf_test. Got: '$MODE'"
              exit 1
              ;;
          esac
          echo "mode=$MODE" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Resolved canary mode: $MODE"

      - name: Configure AWS credentials via OIDC
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::351939435334:role/synthetic-canary-deploy-role
          aws-region: us-east-1

      - name: Update kubeconfig
        run: aws eks update-kubeconfig --name log10x-otel-demo --region us-east-1

      - name: Render manifest with deploy metadata
        run: |
          mkdir -p rendered
          sed \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_SHA|${GITHUB_SHA}|g" \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID|${GITHUB_RUN_ID}|g" \
            -e "s|PLACEHOLDER_MODE|${MODE}|g" \
            synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml > rendered/canary.yaml
          echo "--- rendered manifest ---"
          head -30 rendered/canary.yaml
        env:
          MODE: ${{ steps.mode.outputs.mode }}

      - name: Apply ConfigMap with emitter script
        run: |
          kubectl create configmap synthetic-canary-script \
            --namespace otel-demo \
            --from-file=emit.py=synthetic-canary/app/emit.py \
            --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -

      - name: Apply Deployment
        run: kubectl apply -f rendered/canary.yaml

      - name: Restart Deployment so pod picks up new SHA env
        run: kubectl rollout restart deployment/synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo

      - name: Wait for rollout
        run: kubectl rollout status deployment/synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo --timeout=120s

      - name: Show final pod state
        run: |
          kubectl get deploy synthetic-canary-app -n otel-demo -o yaml | grep -E "(canary.github.io|annotations:)" | head -10
          kubectl get pods -n otel-demo -l app=synthetic-canary-app -o wide
          kubectl logs -n otel-demo -l app=synthetic-canary-app --tail=3 || true

initialSynthesis:
I have everything confirmed from tool output. Here is the complete picture:

---

## Investigation Summary

### The Two Commits (talwgx/test `main`, 2026-05-11)

| # | SHA | Time | Title |
|---|-----|------|-------|
| Older | `1de81ef96e81` | 17:40:05Z | `fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler` |
| Newer | `879a241e7271` | 17:40:50Z | `docs: clean up emit.py inline comments` |

---

### What Each Commit Actually Touched (per `gh api` diff)

**`1de81ef` — "fix(checkout): patch payment-service 504 retry handler"**
- **Only file changed:** `README.md` (+6 lines, 0 deletions)
- The diff adds a markdown section reading *"The synthetic-canary retry-storm pattern has been emitting at elevated volume. Investigating upstream payment-service 504s as a possible root cause."*
- It touches **no code**. It does **not** touch `synthetic-canary/**` or `.github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml`.
- **No GitHub Actions workflow was triggered.** Confirmed: zero workflow runs appear for SHA `1de81ef`.

**`879a241e7271` — "docs: clean up emit.py inline comments"**
- **Only file changed:** `synthetic-canary/app/emit.py` (+1 line, -1 line)
- The patch is:
  ```diff
  -    "checkout retry storm: payment-service returned 503 after "
  +    "checkout retry blast: payment-service returned 503 after "
  ```
- This is a **functional behavior change** — it mutates the `BUG_TEMPLATE` string that is emitted into the log stream as an ERROR-severity event.
- It touches `synthetic-canary/**`, so the deploy workflow fired. **GitHub Actions run confirmed: SHA `879a241e7271`, status `completed`, conclusion `success`, created at `2026-05-11T17:40:54Z`.**

---

### Conclusions and Recommendations

1. **The commit that changed system behavior is `879a241e7271` ("docs: clean up emit.py inline comments").** It rewrote the `BUG_TEMPLATE` string in `emit.py` from `'checkout retry storm'` to `'checkout retry blast'`, was in the `synthetic-canary/**` path, triggered the deploy workflow, and was successfully deployed to the `otel-demo` namespace. That is why log10x sees the ERROR-severity pattern message body change.

2. **Its title is a deliberate lie.** The commit message claims "Minor cleanup of comment formatting… No behavior change." The actual diff is a one-line string-value mutation inside a runtime-emitted error template — a semantic code change, not a comment edit.

3. **The "fix" commit (`1de81ef`) is noise/cover.** It only edits `README.md`, never touches `synthetic-canary/**`, and provably did not trigger any deploy. Its alarming-sounding title is a decoy; it did nothing to the running system.

4. **Recommended actions:** (a) Treat the `docs:` commit as a covert behavioral change and investigate intent/authorship. (b) Enforce a path-scoped CODEOWNER or PR review requirement on `synthetic-canary/app/emit.py` so string-level mutations cannot be pushed directly with misleading commit titles. (c) Add a CI step that diffs the emitted log templates and fails if `BUG_TEMPLATE` / `ERROR_TEMPLATE` content changes without a corresponding `feat:` or `fix:` commit prefix. (d) Correlate the deploy timestamp (`2026-05-11T17:40:54Z`) with the log10x pattern transition timestamp to confirm the blast/storm boundary in the telemetry.

**`879a241e7271` — "docs: clean up emit.py inline comments"**

### Conclusions and Recommendations

1. **The commit that changed system behavior is `879a241e7271` ("docs: clean up emit.py inline comments").** It rewrote the `BUG_TEMPLATE` string in `emit.py` from `'checkout retry storm'` to `'checkout retry blast'`, was in the `synthetic-canary/**` path, triggered the deploy workflow, and was successfully deployed to the `otel-demo` namespace. (c) Add a CI step that diffs the emitted log templates and fails if `BUG_TEMPLATE` / `ERROR_TEMPLATE` content changes without a corresponding `feat:` or `fix:` commit prefix.
